items.title: Teenager killed as motorcycle catches fire after collision
items.summary: PUTATAN: A teenager was killed after the motorcycle he was riding was hit by another vehicle before catching fire along Jalan Putatan early this morning. Putatan Fire and Rescue Station chief Musran Jabri said his department received an emergency call at 4.45am. "At the scene, the victim's motorcycle was found to have been destroyed by fire after being hit by another vehicle.

items.title: Penang eyes Middle East market after RM1.1bil medical tourism revenue
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang is exploring the Middle East as a new medical tourism source market to diversify international patient arrivals beyond Indonesia and Southeast Asia. He said the move would reduce reliance on traditional medical tourism markets and enhance resilience. They serve as a strategic bridge connecting Penang with Qatar and the broader Middle East, opening new opportunities for tourism, business exchanges and, importantly, medical tourism.

items.title: People’s Income Initiative delivers income boost to nearly 7,800 households, ministry says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The People’s Income Initiative (IPR) programme has benefitted 7,787 households after almost three years of implementation, according to the Economy Ministry. “After nearly three years of implementation, the IPR programme has received highly encouraging response and yielded impressive results. Regarding PADU, the ministry said 204 agencies have implemented continuous data sharing and integration with PADU.

items.title: Sony to stop releasing new playstation games on discs from 2028
items.summary: TOKYO: Sony said it will stop releasing new games for its PlayStation consoles on physical discs from 2028, as the company accelerates its shift towards digital distribution, German Press Agency (dpa) reported. From 2028, all new PlayStation titles will be available only in digital form, Sony said on Wednesday. The company cited growing consumer demand for digital purchases, noting that about 80 per cent of games sold for the PlayStation 5 are now bought digitally.

items.title: Malaysians remain Indonesia’s biggest source of foreign tourists in May
items.summary: JAKARTA, July 2 — Malaysia remained the largest source of foreign tourists to Indonesia in May, accounting for 21.58 per cent of all international arrivals, according to Indonesia’s Statistics Agency (BPS). BPS Deputy for Distribution and Services Statistics Ateng Hartono said tourist arrivals from Malaysia increased both month-on-month and year-on-year basis. “The largest number of international tourist arrivals was by tourists from Malaysia (21.58 per cent,) followed by Australia at 11.22 per cent and Singapore at 9.89 per cent,” he said at a press conference, streamed live on YouTube on Wednesday.

items.title: Defence Ministry denies premature payment for Norway missile procurement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Defence Ministry has rejected claims that the government made a premature RM571.9 million payment for the procurement of Norway-made Naval Strike Missile (NSM) systems. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the payment was not an advance payment but was made in accordance with the contract after the supplier completed the required work. Khaled also dismissed claims that the contract between the navy and the supplier lacked clauses to safeguard Malaysia's interests.

items.title: South Korean shops turn to robots, self-service to escape labour woes
items.summary: SEOUL: Unstaffed coffee shops, ramen eateries and flower outlets are spreading across South Korea as owners turn to robots and self-service to overcome rising labour costs in a business model that relies on the widespread honesty of users. Such stores, usually open 24 hours, were estimated to number 9,000 nationwide by the end of 2024, the National Fire Agency said, while payments provider Samsung Card said their number probably grew four times by 2025 from 2020. His company runs eight such 24-hour coffee shops, entirely unstaffed, across South Korea, most of them in Seoul, the capital, although human employees prepare coffee drinks in four more.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: Online Safety Act rules, school safety and West Asia fallout top agenda
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The development of subsidiary instruments under the Online Safety Act 2025 (Act 886), student safety in school and assistance for micro-entrepreneurs affected by the West Asia crisis are among the key issues to be raised in the Dewan Rakyat today. According to the Order Paper, Rodziah Ismail (PH-Ampang) will ask the Communications Minister during Minister’s Question Time to provide details on the 10 subsidiary instruments, including regulations and guidelines, being developed under the Online Safety Act 2025 (Act 886), covering their regulatory objectives, key provisions, scope and latest development status. Datuk Andi Muhammad Suryady Bandy (BN-Kalabakan) will ask the Finance Minister to outline immediate measures to assist small traders, hawkers and micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) affected by rising logistics costs and supply chain disruptions stemming from the prolonged crisis in West Asia.

items.title: Oil falls after US, Iran talks conclude in Doha
items.summary: BEIJING: Oil prices dropped in early trade on Thursday after Qatar said Iran and the US had made "positive progress" in indirect talks that concluded on Wednesday, focused on the Strait of Hormuz, which handled one-fifth of global oil supply before the war. Brent futures were down US$0.73, or 1.02 per cent, to US$70.84 a barrel by 0102 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate crude fell US$0.83, or 1.21 per cent, to US$67.75 a barrel. Sources said negotiators for the US and Iran spent two days in Doha discussing maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz and unfreezing Iran's funds.

items.title: Norway's decision to revoke missile export licence will not delay LCS project, says Khaled
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The revocation of Norway's export licence for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system will not affect the delivery schedule of the Royal Malaysian Navy's (RMN) Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the Defence Ministry, through the RMN, was evaluating potential missile systems to replace the NSM while ensuring the LCS programme remained on track. To ensure Malaysia's maritime defence readiness remained intact, Khaled said the ministry had implemented several mitigation measures, including continuing life extension and maintenance programmes for existing assets, diversifying defence procurement sources and strengthening defence diplomacy to ensure the continuity of strategic supply chains.

items.title: Stolen to order: Police bust alleged bonded lorry theft ring linked to RM1.23m in losses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Police arrested seven individuals, including the mastermind, believed to be involved in a bonded lorry theft syndicate active in Selangor, Johor, Melaka, and Kuala Lumpur last week. Investigations revealed one suspect is the mastermind, acting as a middleman to recruit thieves, illegal storage operators, mechanics, runners, and buyers of stolen lorry components,” he said in a statement yesterday. Kumar said the syndicate is believed to be involved in about 18 bonded lorry theft cases in Johor, Melaka, Selangor, and Kuala Lumpur, with losses estimated at RM1,230,000.

items.title: Five Chinese citizens injured after GPS leads SUV into 7m-deep construction site
items.summary: MACHANG: Five Chinese nationals were injured after their sports utility vehicle (SUV) plunged into a seven-metre-deep culvert construction site at Km41 of Jalan Kota Baru-Kuala Krai at midnight yesterday. District police chief Superintendent Azuhar Md Nor said initial investigations revealed the accident occurred at 11.50pm when the Toyota Prius driven by a 66-year-old Chinese citizen was heading from Temangan, Machang, towards Tok Bali, Pasir Puteh. "The police received a report regarding the incident from members of the public at 12.01am," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Dollar steady as focus turns to US payrolls, yen intervention jitters persist
items.summary: TOKYO: The dollar held steady on Thursday as markets awaited key US non-farm payrolls data, while the yen's slide to 40-year lows against the greenback and thin trading ahead of a US holiday kept traders on high alert for intervention. The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, eased 0.02 per cent to 101.38. The euro traded at US$1.138 against the US dollar while sterling edged 0.06 per cent higher to US$1.3279.

items.title: Drivers are turning to syabu and ketum to stay awake — and putting lives at risk, says anti-drug agency
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 2 — Methamphetamine, or syabu, and ketum abuse has become an emerging trend among drivers, including commercial vehicle operators, over the past five years, posing a greater risk to road safety. He said this was evident from the integrated anti-drug operation involving commercial vehicle drivers conducted nationwide yesterday, during which 244 drivers were screened and 22, aged between 21 and 61, were arrested after testing positive for syabu, ketum and ganja. “Most drivers take these substances to stay energised.

items.title: As Super El Nino looms, experts urge Malaysian parents to take heat risks to children seriously
items.summary: The 41-year-old mother of three, whose children are aged between four and 10, recalled how her second child, eight-year-old Muhammad Irfan Sufian Muhammad Noor, suffered heat exhaustion last year after prolonged exposure to the midday sun during his school’s sports day. He also said that prolonged exposure to intense heat and direct sunlight could have progressed to heat stroke,” she said. Even being inside a car can increase the risk of heat exhaustion and heat stroke if the body isn’t getting enough fluids,” she said.

items.title: #NSTviral: 'Are you from Malaysia too?' Cable car encounter in Vietnam delights netizens
items.summary: The video, shared on Reddit by user "Usernamegenerik" two days ago and reposted across multiple social media platforms, has drawn widespread attention for its wholesome exchange. Many social media users said the encounter reflected the unique bond Malaysians often share when meeting fellow countrymen overseas. Reddit user Itsme-htan commented "And a beautiful new friendship between fellow Malaysians is born."

items.title: Ringgit opens slightly firmer against greenback as US rate concerns ease
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened slightly firmer against the US dollar on Thursday as easing concerns over the United States' monetary policy outlook supported sentiment after remarks by US Federal Reserve (Fed) officials on interest rates. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said market focus was on comments by new Fed chair Kevin Warsh at the European Central Bank (ECB) Forum, which suggested the US central bank was in no hurry to adjust interest rates while remaining committed to restoring price stability. Mohd Afzanizam said softer-than-expected US economic data also supported the ringgit.

items.title: Experts: Super EL-Nino raises health risks for Malaysia's children
items.summary: The 41-year-old mother of three, whose children are aged between four and 10, recalled how her second child, eight-year-old Muhammad Irfan Sufian Muhammad Noor, suffered heat exhaustion last year after prolonged exposure to the midday sun during his school's sports day. "He also said that prolonged exposure to intense heat and direct sunlight could have progressed to heat stroke," she said. "Even being inside a car can increase the risk of heat exhaustion and heat stroke if the body isn't getting enough fluids," she said.

items.title: US in talks with AI companies for voluntary model standards, FT reports
items.summary: BENGALURU: The US government is in advanced talks with AI companies to create voluntary standards for the release of new models, with an announcement possible as soon as next week, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday, citing sources. The standards would set benchmarks for advanced models and timelines, while clarifying who can access them in the US and abroad, according to the FT report. Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

items.title: Pakatan shrugs off PAS call to back BN in Johor seats it isn't contesting
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 2 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) election machinery is unfazed by PAS’ directive instructing its supporters to vote for Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in seats the party is not contesting in the Johor state election, Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said. He said PH would continue campaigning as planned and would not be distracted by any provocation or strategy employed by its opponents. “The PH machinery will continue campaigning as usual despite the directive.

items.title: Russia strikes Ukraine capital with missiles and drones, wounds 5
items.summary: KVIY, Ukraine: Russian missile and drone strikes rocked Kyiv early on Thursday, setting off fires and wounding at least five people, after President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned that Moscow was preparing a "massive attack." The attack came after the Ukrainian air force warned that ballistic missiles were headed towards the capital and followed Zelenskyy cutting short a visit to Dublin Wednesday, citing intelligence reports of an impending Russian strike. "Kyiv is under attack from ballistic missiles and UAVs," Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said on Telegram, adding that blasts could be heard across the city.

items.title: Passenger safely rescued after falling onto Hang Tuah LRT tracks, services resume within 16 minutes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — A passenger was rescued after allegedly fainting and falling onto the tracks at Rapid KL’s Hang Tuah LRT station yesterday evening, prompting a brief suspension of train services on the Ampang and Sri Petaling lines. In a statement issued last night, Rapid KL said the incident occurred at 6.36pm at Hang Tuah station, where the passenger was believed to have fainted before losing balance and falling onto the tracks. Rapid KL said passenger safety remained its highest priority and reminded commuters to observe station safety rules.

items.title: Palestine rejects attempts to undermine UNRWA's mandate in Gaza
items.summary: RAMALLAH, (West Bank): Palestine rejected attempts yesterday to undermine the mandate of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA), reaffirming that the agency remains "an indispensable lifeline" for Palestinians, Anadolu Ajansi reported. In a statement, the Palestinian Foreign Ministry said UNRWA plays an "essential role" in providing education, healthcare, social protection and emergency assistance across the occupied Palestinian territories, including East Jerusalem and the Gaza Strip, as well as in refugee camps in neighbouring host countries. The ministry stressed that UNRWA was established under a UN mandate and operates in accordance with international law.

items.title: Pakatan pledges high-paying jobs to reduce Johoreans’ reliance on Singapore work
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 2 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) intends to offer more high-paying job opportunities in Johor through better-structured and more strategic economic planning for the state. PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said one of PH’s main priorities is to ensure that Johoreans are no longer dependent on jobs in Singapore, requiring them to cross the Johor Causeway every day to earn a living. The event also featured PH leaders including Parti Amanah Negara’s (Amanah) Datuk Seri Mujahid Rawa, PKR’s Hassan Abdul Karim and DAP’s Young Syefura.

items.title: Palestinian goalkeeper Saleem Al-Ashqar killed by Israel army in Gaza
items.summary: ATLANTA: Palestinian goalkeeper Saleem Al-Ashqar was killed earlier this week in the Gaza Strip by the Israeli army, Anadolu Ajansi reported citing a statement by the Palestinian Football Association (PFA). It said he was one of hundreds of Palestinian sporting figures killed, including more than 1,000 Palestinian athletes, since the genocidal war began in Oct 2023. "We deeply mourn the tragic death of 32-year-old Palestinian goalkeeper Salim Al-Ashqar.

items.title: South Korea's inflation rate hits more than two year high
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea's consumer inflation accelerated to a two-and-a-half-year high in June, cementing expectations for an interest rate hike by the central bank as early as its upcoming policy meeting on July 16. The consumer price index (CPI) rose 3.2 per cent from a year earlier, marking the biggest jump since December 2023 after accelerating from 3.1 per cent in May, according to the Ministry of Data and Statistics. Five of the seven members on the Bank of Korea's monetary policy board voted to keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 2.50 per cent on May 28, while two dissenters voted for a 25-basis-point hike.

items.title: 'Everybody's profiting': Trump defends US$1bil crypto earnings
items.summary: JOINT BASE ANDREWS, United States: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday defended earning US$1.2 billion from his family's cryptocurrency activities last year, saying "everybody's profiting" from his time in power. "You know why I'm profiting, because the stock market's going up, everybody's profiting," Trump told reporters at Joint Base Andrews near Washington. "I don't get involved in my personal (finances), we have funds that run my money," Trump said.

items.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll hits 2,295 with over 11,000 injured
items.summary: BOGOTA, (Colombia): The confirmed death toll from the twin earthquakes that struck northern Venezuela on June 24 has reached 2,295, National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez announced on Wednesday, Anadolu Ajansi reported. In a national briefing broadcast on state television channel Venezolana de Television (VTV), Rodriguez detailed the widening scale of the disaster, noting that the number of documented injuries has climbed to 11,267, while official displacement figures now stand at 12,841 people. "Hope remains intact," Rodriguez said.

items.title: Why shareholders change external auditors?
items.summary: Independence One of the primary reasons shareholders may change external auditors is concern over auditor independence. In such circumstances, shareholders may support a change of auditor to bring in a fresh and independent perspective. Performance Shareholders may also seek a change when there are concerns regarding the auditor's performance.

items.title: HLIB upgrades PetChem to 'Buy' on growth plans, asset sale
items.summary: The company aims to increase the contribution of its specialty chemicals business to more than 30 per cent of revenue by 2030 from 20 per cent in fiscal 2025. On the company's olefins and derivatives (O&D) business, he said polyethylene (PE) prices had fallen about 40 per cent from recent highs, while naphtha prices had declined a steeper 47 per cent, returning largely to pre-conflict levels. As a result, PE-naphtha spreads remained healthy at around US$600 to US$800 per tonne.

items.title: OMV reprieve offers relief but not resolution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The six-month deferment of the revised open market value (OMV) calculation for locally-assembled (CKD) vehicles underscores the need for a permanent tax framework instead of repeated short-term extensions. Automotive analyst Shamsul Yunos said the latest extension would help avoid immediate disruption to the automotive industry. The revised OMV methodology, first gazetted in 2019, seeks to broaden the basis for calculating excise duties by including certain non-manufacturing costs such as marketing expenses, administrative costs and profits, in vehicle valuations.

items.title: Big wildfires hit southern France
items.summary: OUPIA, France: A large wildfire raged in southern France on Wednesday, forcing scores of people to be evacuated, emergency services said. Hundreds of firefighters mobilised to battle the blaze in the Herault and Aude departments, fuelled by drought and strong winds. By late evening the fire had covered 800 hectares, according to the prefecture.

items.title: US signs agreement to build permanent embassy in Jerusalem
items.summary: JERUSALEM: The United States on Wednesday signed an agreement to build a new embassy compound in Jerusalem, a move that Israel said reflected the "unbreakable alliance" between the countries. During his first term, US President Donald Trump recognised Jerusalem as Israel's capital in December 2017 and ordered the relocation of Washington's diplomatic mission from Tel Aviv. US ambassador Mike Huckabee said during a signing ceremony at Israel's foreign ministry that Washington "recognises Jerusalem as the eternal, indigenous, and forever capital of the Jewish people."

items.title: Trump sees progress as US, Iran hold Qatar talks
items.summary: DOHA: US President Donald Trump said Wednesday that indirect talks with Iran in Qatar were making progress, offering a tentative sign that diplomacy was holding after recent exchanges of fire threatened efforts to end the Middle East war. Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi, who led Tehran's delegation, later said the talks had concluded and that the sides had agreed to establish a communication channel by Thursday to report and record violations of their initial memorandum of understanding. Iran had insisted there would be no direct negotiations in Doha on the deal, which aims to end the war that began with US-Israeli strikes on Iran in late February.

items.title: 'Advanced AI models akin to nukes'
items.summary: CENTRAL Intelligence Agency (CIA) director John Ratcliffe on Tuesday compared the capabilities of the most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models with nuclear weapons, in a tacit defence of the Trump administration's recent hard line on controlling the release of the most powerful AI technology. OpenAI, Anthropic's American rival, launched its GPT-5.6 model the same day with very limited access, agreeing for the first time to let the US government vet authorised partners on a client-by-client basis. The analogy between cutting-edge AI and nuclear weapons has become increasingly common in US national security circles, where several think tanks describe a genuine technological "arms race" pitting the US against China and Russia.

items.title: Ex-soldiers take on MRSM warden roles to build students' character [WATCH]
items.summary: MUAR: Fostering spiritual values and personal development among students are the main objectives of two former soldiers who began their duties as wardens at Muar Mara Junior Science College (MRSM) here. Muhammad Faridzal Wasimin, 49, and Mohd Nazrin Zarkah, 43, are determined to guide the more than 700 students at the college. They are among 16 former military personnel who reported for duty as wardens at eight MRSM yesterday.

items.title: Johor polls: Early edge for BN as Johor campaign gathers pace
items.summary: BN will go head-to-head with its unity government partner Pakatan Harapan in all 56 seats, aiming to defend and improve on the 40 seats the Umno-led coalition won in the 2022 state polls. Political analyst Associate Professor Dr Syaza Shukri said Pas' decision to contest only 11 seats put BN in the driver's seat and would likely help the coalition secure a clear mandate. "Can BN confidently secure 29 seats?

items.title: Seven things you need to know about Malaysia’s improved AG-PP Bill, including prosecutor’s seven-year cap
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — This month, the government’s proposed new law to split the Attorney General (AG) and Public Prosecutor (PP) into two separate jobs is expected to return to Parliament. A 12-member parliamentary special select committee (PSSC), led by the law minister and including government and opposition MPs, had on June 22 released their proposed improved version of the Bill. Here’s a quick summary of new improved version of the Bill as proposed by the PSSC: 1.

items.title: MACC officer selected for prestigious FBI course
items.summary: A SENIOR Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) officer has been selected for the prestigious Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) National Academy training programme in the United States. MACC Special Operations Division Senior Assistant Commissioner Irawan Khlir is the third officer from the commission to be selected for the international training programme in Quantico, Virginia, from July 8 to Sept 17. The programme aims to enhance officer competency in strategic leadership, operational management, advanced investigation techniques, high-tech intelligence and handling cross-border security threats, it said in a statement.

items.title: 'More than 20,000 turtle nests now recorded yearly'
items.summary: TURTLE Islands Park in Sandakan is one of the world's longest-running sea turtle conservation programmes, with nesting numbers having risen sharply since the 1970s, said a researcher. Universiti Malaysia Sabah researcher Associate Professor Dr Juanita Joseph said the site had been run even longer than similar conservation programmes in the United States and was also recognised internationally as an outstanding conservation model. The most common turtle species in Sabah is the green turtle.

items.title: Anwar hopes book on his ideas will serve as reference for future generations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim hopes that the book, titled Gagasan Anwar Ibrahim Dalam Peradaban Budaya Bangsa , which compiles his ideas, views and life experiences, will serve as a reference for future generations, particularly on nation-building and character development. Anwar also expressed appreciation for the contribution of eminent language scholar Professor Emeritus Datuk Dr Nik Safiah Abdul Karim, who served as the book’s academic adviser during its preparation and development. He said he was also interviewed exclusively by the two scholars to ensure the book comprehensively covers its intended content.

items.title: Khazanah backs PAC recommendations to strengthen Malaysia’s airport ecosystem
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Khazanah Nasional Bhd has welcomed the recommendations of the Public Accounts Committee (PAC) on Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) and the continued strengthening of Malaysia’s airport ecosystem. “The PAC’s recommendations reinforce the broader objective that guided the privatisation of MAHB, which is to strengthen Malaysia’s aviation sector by improving connectivity, enhancing passenger experience and reinforcing the competitiveness of our airports. Managing director Datuk Amirul Feisal Wan Zahir said that the objective of taking MAHB private was about creating the conditions for long-term transformation by enabling sustained investment, operational excellence and continuous improvement across Malaysia’s airport network.

items.title: Policeman killed on North-South Expressway near Seberang Jaya after motorcycle hits tyre on road
items.summary: Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng said the 40-year-old victim, who was attached to the Perlis Police Contingent Headquarters (IPK), died at the scene from severe head injuries after his motorcycle crashed into the tyre. He said police received a report at 6 am about the accident involving a car and a motorcycle, where initial investigations revealed that the car, travelling from Juru towards Seberang Jaya, is believed to have struck the tyre in the middle of the road. Lim said the car driver and two passengers were injured and sent to Seberang Jaya Hospital for treatment.

items.title: N. Sembilan PKR submits candidate list for 16 state seats, says Aminuddin
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 1 — Negeri Sembilan Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) has submitted a list of names of prospective candidates for the 16 seats it will contest in the 16th state election to the central leadership for consideration. Negeri Sembilan PKR chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said the list includes at least three to five names per seat, comprising women, young candidates and experienced contenders. On June 13, Aminuddin, who is also the Menteri Besar, said the allocation of the 36 Negeri Sembilan state assembly seats sees PKR contesting 16 seats, DAP 11 seats and Amanah the remaining nine.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat passes Cybercrime Bill to strengthen enforcement and protect digital users in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The Dewan Rakyat has passed the Cybercrime Bill, marking a significant step in Malaysia’s efforts to strengthen its legal and enforcement framework against increasingly complex online threats. The Bill was passed with a majority vote following debate by 48 government and opposition Members of Parliament today. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the legislation represents a “game changer” that ensures the country’s legal system remains relevant amid rapid technological advancement, while enhancing protection for the public against cybercrime.

items.title: Sabah sees surge in Chinese tourist arrivals amid expanded Shanghai-Kota Kinabalu flights
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 1 — Sabah is recording a sharp increase in tourist arrivals from China, driven by rising travel demand and expanded flight capacity between Shanghai and Kota Kinabalu. Sabah Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Jafry Ariffin said that confidence in Sabah’s tourism industry is still growing as visitor arrivals from China reached 292,396 between January and April this year, up from 195,147 in the same period last year. China remains Sabah’s biggest international tourism market.

items.title: Bukit Aman moves to profile drivers, companies and vehicles in drug- and drunk-driving crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The Bukit Aman Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) will develop a profiling system involving drivers, companies and commercial vehicles to strengthen efforts to curb drug- and alcohol-impaired driving. Bukit Aman JSPT Director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the initiative is being refined as part of proposals to tighten enforcement, including ensuring companies that employ such drivers are also held accountable for offences committed. “Nearly 90 per cent of those detained were commercial vehicle drivers, involving 121 lorry drivers and 14 others,” he said.

items.title: MBSA: LRT3 pedestrian walkways were required under 2019 approvals for Prasarana and developers
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 1 — The Shah Alam City Council (MBSA) today said pedestrian walkways linking LRT3 stations to surrounding developments had been incorporated into development plans approved as early as 2019, following public concerns over incomplete access routes after the line began operations. In a statement, MBSA said it had required pedestrian walkways connecting the stations to nearby commercial developments, public facilities and activity centres as part of the planning approval process for the LRT3 project. “Accordingly, when approving the development plans for the LRT3 stations in 2019, MBSA imposed conditions requiring the provision of pedestrian walkways connecting the stations to surrounding activity centres, commercial developments and public facilities,” it said.

items.title: Johor polls: Onn Hafiz rejects uneven development claims, cites state economic transformation plan for balanced growth
items.summary: MUAR, July 1 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has dismissed claims that development in the state is uneven and has forced residents to migrate elsewhere, stressing that development across all districts is guided by a comprehensive plan. Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, explained that under the Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP), the state’s development framework is planned comprehensively, with strategic emphasis placed on equitably addressing the unique socioeconomic priorities of each district. Also present was BN candidate for the Maharani state seat, Datuk Ashari Md Sarip.

items.title: High Court dismisses Semantan Estate bid to postpone Duta Enclave compensation hearing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The High Court today dismissed an application by Semantan Estate (1952) Sdn Bhd to stay proceedings to determine the amount of damages to be paid by the government regarding the value of the land known as Duta Enclave. On May 18, Semantan Estate filed an application to stay the proceedings until the appeal to obtain several documents including land acquisition plans between 1956 and 1960 to be used in determining the amount of damages to be paid by the government in relation to the ownership of the Duta Enclave land is decided by the Court of Appeal. On November 13 2025, the Federal Court dismissed Semantan Estate’s application for leave to appeal against the Court of Appeal’s decision that the Federal Territory Land Titles Registrar did not need to register 263.272 acres of land in Duta Enclave in the name of the company.

items.title: Pahang police seize over RM728,000 in drugs, arrest couple believed to be East Coast couriers
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 1 — Police arrested a couple and seized various types of drugs estimated to be worth more than RM728,000 at a hotel in Genting Permai, Bentong, on Monday. Acting Pahang police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the suspects, a 37-year-old local man and a 35-year-old foreign woman, were arrested by a police team at the hotel’s parking area at about 5pm. He said searches of a hotel room and a car led to the discovery of various types of drugs, including an estimated 2.9 kilogrammes (kg) of ketamine, 1.01 kg of cocaine, 671 grammes of MDMA powder, and 538 grammes of methamphetamine.

items.title: In Perak, prosecution rejects bid to drop charge against woman accused of trying to assault Sultan Nazrin
items.summary: IPOH, July 1 — The prosecution has rejected a representation by a woman seeking to have a charge of using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, during the state-level National Day celebration last year withdrawn. Deputy Public Prosecutor Nasrul Hadi Abdul Ghani informed the court of the decision during the mention of the case involving Nurhaswani Afni Mohamad Zorki, 41, before Magistrate Norizan Ridzuan, who fixed July 6 for case management. On October 8 last year, the same court rejected Nurhaswani Afni’s guilty plea after the prosecution informed the court that a report from Hospital Bahagia Tanjung Rambutan in Ulu Kinta here stated that the accused suffered from schizophrenia and was of unsound mind at the time of the incident.

items.title: Sarawak retiree loses RM914,000 to fake AI investment scam promising quick returns
items.summary: MUKAH, July 1 — A 56-year-old government retiree lost more than RM914,000 after being lured into a bogus online investment scheme that promised lucrative returns through an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered trading system. Mukah police chief DSP Muhamad Rizal Alias said police received a report from the victim at about 2.20pm yesterday. “The suspects claimed the investment relied on an AI-powered automated trading system capable of generating exceptionally high returns within a short period, convincing the victim to continue investing,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Powering AI: Data centres set to consume nearly a third of Malaysia’s electricity by 2035
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Electricity consumption by the data centre sector is expected to increase significantly to 31 per cent — equivalent to 73,274 gigawatt-hours (GWh) — by 2035, up from 7 per cent or 10,544 GWh in 2026. The Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation said the figures are based on projections by the Electricity Supply and Tariff Planning and Implementation Committee (JPPPET). According to the ministry, the rapid growth of the artificial intelligence (AI) and data centre industries has led to an increase in the country’s peak electricity demand.

items.title: Khaled: Malaysia to begin rocket production within two years
items.summary: CANBERRA, July 1 — Malaysia is set to begin rocket production within the next two years, with plans to eventually expand into missile manufacturing, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. Mohamed Khaled added that Weststar’s investment will also allow Malaysia to plug into RTI’s global supply chain, opening doors for local players to thrive within the international defence ecosystem. Mohamed Khaled said the move is also in line with the National Defence Industry Policy, which aims to strengthen local defence capabilities and achieve sovereign defence standing.

items.title: Selangor Sultan credits Najib for LRT3, says project restored under Anwar, faults Lim Guan Eng over project cuts
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 1 — The Sultan of Selangor today credited former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak with approving the Shah Alam LRT3 project at his request in 2015, said the subsequent Pakatan Harapan administration had reduced its scope, and praised Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for restoring key elements of the project. In a statement issued to mark the opening of the Shah Alam LRT3 line, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah said the rail project was conceived to ease chronic traffic congestion faced by commuters travelling between Klang, Shah Alam and Kuala Lumpur. “First and foremost, I would like to give credit to former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak for heeding my decree that a rail transport facility be built connecting Klang, Shah Alam and Kuala Lumpur,” the Sultan said.

items.title: Police rule out foul play in death of autistic boy found in Perak drain
items.summary: IPOH, July 1 — Police have ruled out foul play in the death of a six-year-old autistic boy who was found in a drain at Kampung Baru Sungai Sumun, Hutan Melintang, yesterday. Bagan Datuk District Police chief Supt Mohammad Ali Mohd Jali said the conclusion was based on the post-mortem conducted by a forensic pathologist at Hospital Teluk Intan, which confirmed that the cause of death was drowning. Commenting on the victim’s disappearance, Mohammad Ali said police received a report on the incident at about 4.54 pm after the child was last seen in the living room of his house in Kampung Baru Sungai Sumun.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: LRT3 shows government commitment to modern, integrated public transport
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 1 — The Shah Alam Line LRT3 is another testament to the Madani government’s commitment to providing a more modern, efficient and integrated public transport system to improve people’s daily lives, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He said the new line was introduced to ease congestion along one of the busiest transport corridors in the Klang Valley. On Sunday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, when launching operations of the line, announced free fares on the Shah Alam Line LRT3 from June 29 to July 31, including Prasarana Malaysia Bhd (Prasarana) feeder bus services along the route.

items.title: Border Control and Protection Agency seeks Glock pistols for officers, cites durability and low maintenance
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 — The Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) has proposed the procurement of Glock firearms for its personnel, citing the weapon’s durability and low maintenance costs. AKPS director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain said the government has given its in-principle approval to supply the agency with firearms to facilitate operations and ensure safety during duty. Earlier, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had approved an allocation of RM22 million to equip AKPS with suitable firearms and equipment.

items.title: Heartbreaking scenes as mother elephant refuses to leave dead calf after fatal road collision in Mersing (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — An adult elephant believed to be the mother of a calf killed in a pre-dawn road collision remained at the crash site in Johor this morning, staying nearby until wildlife officers completed an operation to bury the young elephant and return it safely to the jungle. According to New Straits Times , the elephant calf was struck by a car along Jalan Felda Nitar near Kampung Pengkalan Bukit Asli, in Mersing. An adult elephant lingered at the scene, disrupting traffic as rescuers and wildlife personnel responded.
